notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: Sddc lifecycle exposes CREATING, UPDATING, ACTIVE, DELETING, DELETED, and FAILED. Repo-authored controller success is ACTIVE, requeue covers CREATING and UPDATING, FAILED is terminal without requeue, and delete confirmation follows DELETING to DELETED or NotFound.

Pinned OCI SDK contract: UpdateSddcDetails exposes displayName, vmwareSoftwareVersion, esxiSoftwareVersion, sshAuthorizedKeys, freeformTags, and definedTags as the in-place update surface. Fields absent from UpdateSddcDetails remain create-only drift and never open implicit replacement.

CreateSddc and DeleteSddc return opc-work-request-id headers, while UpdateSddc returns the Sddc body directly. The reviewed generatedruntime client records request and work-request breadcrumbs when present and relies on lifecycle projection plus confirm-delete rereads rather than service-local work-request polling.

ListSddcsRequest filters by compartmentId, displayName, lifecycleState, and computeAvailabilityDomain and returns Items []SddcSummary. Repo-authored bind logic only attempts pre-create reuse when spec.displayName is set, and safe reusable matching is exact compartment_id plus display_name in reusable ACTIVE, CREATING, or UPDATING states; duplicate exact matches fail instead of guessing.
